medications.indication_rationale: Start within ~72h of rash onset to speed healing and reduce postherpetic neuralgia, especially in an older adult.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g., valaciclovir 1 g three times daily for 7 days (as per source note; adjust for renal function)
contraindications_in_this_case: Adjust dose in renal impairment; ensure hydration

medications.indication_rationale: Zoster neuralgia often needs neuropathic agents; simple analgesia alone is insufficient. Titrate cautiously in an older adult.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g., pregabalin 75 mg at night, titrated (as per source note; per prescriber)
contraindications_in_this_case: Sedation/falls risk and renal dosing in older adults — start low, titrate

medications.drug_name: sticky occlusive dressings/creams on the rash
medications.indication_rationale: Keep lesions dry and uncovered to promote crusting; occlusive dressings are not advised.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the rash clean, dry and uncovered; keep away from pregnant women and non-immune infants until all lesions have crusted; take deep breaths despite the pain to prevent chest complications.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces secondary infection and transmission, and prevents hypoventilation atelectasis.

patient_education_points.point: This is shingles — the chickenpox virus reactivating in one nerve on your right chest. That's why the pain felt like a heart problem before the rash appeared. The antiviral works best started now, within the first few days.

Standard painkillers often aren't enough for this nerve pain, so we add a specific nerve-pain medicine. Keep the rash dry and uncovered and stay away from pregnant women and babies until it crusts over.

If the pain becomes crushing or spreads to your arm/jaw with sweating, you become truly breathless or develop a productive cough, or the rash spreads all over your body — call 000 / go to the emergency department.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Pain control (postherpetic neuralgia risk), lesion healing, and any cardiac/respiratory or ophthalmic concerns.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Cardiac/respiratory red flags or disseminated/ophthalmic zoster — emergency care.

must_include_items: Diagnose dermatomal herpes zoster and start antivirals promptly (within ~72h)
Screen for cardiac/respiratory causes of the chest pain and retain a safety-net (no ECG remotely)
Lesion care/transmission advice and a dissemination/ophthalmic safety-net
errors_of_omission: No antivirals or missed the window
Only simple analgesia for severe neuralgia
errors_of_commission: Attributing chest pain solely to zoster and dismissing cardiac risk without screening
High-dose neuropathic agents without cautious titration in an older adult
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires prompt antivirals + neuropathic analgesia + a cardiac/respiratory safety-net; missing ACS screening or the antiviral window are key failures.
